title: Diplacus parryi
intro: Diplacus parryi, also known as Mexican Monkey Flower, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Diplacus parryi include flavonoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, as well as phenolic acids and volatile oils. In herbal medicine, Diplacus parryi is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or topical cream to take advantage of its potential therapeutic properties.
taxonomy: Diplacus parryi is a species of monkey flower. It belongs to the family Phrymaceae. This flowering plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Diplacus parryi is also known as the Parry's monkey flower, and it is a perennial herb that grows in dry to moist areas. It is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Diplacus.
